User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account in the CareerCraft AI application, managing their profile and subscription details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

name.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for login and notifications.

plan.description: The subscription plan of the user (e.g., 'free', 'premium').

onboardingStep.description: Current step in the user onboarding process, guiding them through initial setup.

Resume.title: Resume
Resume.description: Represents a user's master resume or a targeted version, including extracted data and references to detailed sections.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Resume entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this resume. (Relationship: User 1:N Resume)

title.description: A descriptive title for the resume (e.g., 'Master Resume', 'Software Engineer Resume for Google').

createdDate.description: Timestamp when the resume was first created.

updatedDate.description: Timestamp when the resume was last updated.

sourceFileUrl.description: URL to the original uploaded resume file (Word Doc or PDF) in Firebase Storage, if applicable.

candidateName.description: The name of the candidate as extracted from the resume document or provided by the user.

extractedRole.description: The primary role or desired job title extracted from the resume document or determined by AI.

noticePeriod.description: The candidate's notice period as extracted from the resume or specified by the user.

desiredSalary.description: The candidate's desired salary as extracted from the resume or specified by the user.

pcSpecs.description: Specific PC or system specifications mentioned in the resume (e.g., for IT roles), if extracted by AI.

summary.description: A concise, 3-sentence summary of the candidate's profile, often AI-generated or extracted from the resume.

workExperienceIds.description: References to WorkExperience entries associated with this resume. (Relationship: Resume 1:N WorkExperience)

educationIds.description: References to Education entries associated with this resume. (Relationship: Resume 1:N Education)

skillIds.description: References to Skill entries associated with this resume. (Relationship: Resume 1:N Skill)

WorkExperience.title: WorkExperience
WorkExperience.description: Details of a single work experience entry on a resume, designed to be referenced by a Resume entity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorkExperience entity.

resumeId.description: Reference to the Resume this work experience belongs to. (Relationship: Resume 1:N WorkExperience)

jobTitle.description: The title of the job position held.

companyName.description: The name of the company or organization.

location.description: The location (city, state, country) of the employment.

startDate.description: The start date of employment (e.g., 'YYYY-MM' or 'YYYY-MM-DD').

endDate.description: The end date of employment, or 'Present' if currently employed (e.g., 'YYYY-MM' or 'YYYY-MM-DD').

description.description: An array of bullet points describing responsibilities, achievements, and contributions.

order.description: The display order of this experience within its parent resume.

Education.title: Education
Education.description: Details of a single educational entry on a resume, designed to be referenced by a Resume entity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Education entity.

resumeId.description: Reference to the Resume this education entry belongs to. (Relationship: Resume 1:N Education)

institutionName.description: The name of the educational institution.

degree.description: The degree, diploma, or certificate obtained (e.g., 'B.Sc.', 'M.A.').

fieldOfStudy.description: The major or field of study pursued.

location.description: The location (city, state, country) of the institution.

startDate.description: The start date of the educational period (e.g., 'YYYY-MM').

endDate.description: The end date of the educational period, or 'Present' if ongoing (e.g., 'YYYY-MM').

gpa.description: Grade Point Average or equivalent academic metric (e.g., '3.8/4.0', 'First Class Honours').

description.description: Relevant coursework, honors, scholarships, or other academic achievements.

order.description: The display order of this education entry within its parent resume.

Skill.title: Skill
Skill.description: Represents a single skill or competency on a resume, designed to be referenced by a Resume entity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Skill entity.

resumeId.description: Reference to the Resume this skill belongs to. (Relationship: Resume 1:N Skill)

name.description: The name of the skill (e.g., 'JavaScript', 'Project Management', 'Communication').

proficiency.description: The level of proficiency in the skill (e.g., 'Expert', 'Intermediate', 'Beginner', 'Proficient').

category.description: The category of the skill (e.g., 'Programming Languages', 'Soft Skills', 'Tools & Technologies').

order.description: The display order of this skill within its parent resume.

InterviewAnalysis.title: InterviewAnalysis
InterviewAnalysis.description: Stores the structured results of an AI-powered interview transcript analysis, linked to a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InterviewAnalysis entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who initiated this interview analysis. (Relationship: User 1:N InterviewAnalysis)

interviewDate.description: The date and time the interview took place or when the analysis was generated.

transcriptUrl.description: URL to the original interview transcript file (e.g., text, audio) stored in Firebase Storage.

interviewerName.description: The name of the interviewer identified in the transcript, if applicable.

candidateName.description: The name of the candidate identified in the transcript or linked from the user's profile.

overallAssessment.description: An overall summary or assessment of the interview performance generated by the AI.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this interview analysis record was created.

questionAnswerIds.description: References to InterviewQuestionAnswer entities for detailed Q&A pairs. (Relationship: InterviewAnalysis 1:N InterviewQuestionAnswer)

InterviewQuestionAnswer.title: InterviewQuestionAnswer
InterviewQuestionAnswer.description: Stores a single recruitment question and its extracted answer from an interview analysis.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InterviewQuestionAnswer entity.

interviewAnalysisId.description: Reference to the InterviewAnalysis this Q&A pair belongs to. (Relationship: InterviewAnalysis 1:N InterviewQuestionAnswer)

question.description: The specific recruitment question asked during the interview.

answer.description: The candidate's answer to the question, extracted from the transcript by AI.

order.description: The display order of this question/answer pair within its parent interview analysis.

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Records details of a payment transaction made by a user for subscription plans or features.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Payment entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made the payment. (Relationship: User 1:N Payment)

amount.description: The amount of the payment in the smallest currency unit (e.g., cents).

plan.description: The subscription plan or feature purchased (e.g., 'premium', 'pro-addon').

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the payment occurred or was processed.

AIRequest.title: AIRequest
AIRequest.description: Logs requests made to AI services and their results for auditing and debugging across different application features.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AIRequest entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who initiated the AI request. (Relationship: User 1:N AIRequest)

associatedEntityId.description: Reference to the ID of the entity this AI request is associated with (e.g., ResumeId, InterviewAnalysisId). (Relationship: AIRequest 1:1 Resume or AIRequest 1:1 InterviewAnalysis)

associatedEntityType.description: The type of entity this AI request is associated with (e.g., 'Resume', 'InterviewAnalysis').

type.description: The specific type of AI operation performed (e.g., 'suggestSummary', 'extractCVData', 'analyzeInterview', 'matchJobSpec').

inputReference.description: A URL or reference to the input data provided to the AI (e.g., sourceFileUrl for extractCVData, transcriptUrl for analyzeInterview).

resultSummary.description: A brief summary or key outcome of the AI's processing, if available.

fullResult.description: The complete structured result or raw output received from the AI service, stored as a string (e.g., JSON string).

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the AI request was made or completed.

definition.description: Stores user profile and subscription details. The document ID (`userId`) must match the `id` field within the document and the authenticated user's UID (`request.auth.uid`).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, derived from Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores a user's master or targeted resume, including extracted data and references to detailed sections. The document ID (`resumeId`) must match the `id` field within the document. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, which must match the path's `userId` segment.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this resume.

params.name: resumeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the resume document.

definition.description: Details of a single work experience entry belonging to a specific resume. The document ID (`workExperienceId`) must match the `id` field within the document. To ensure authorization independence, documents in this collection MUST include denormalized 'userId' and 'resumeId' fields, matching the respective path segments.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent resume.

params.name: resumeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent resume document.

params.name: workExperienceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the work experience entry.

definition.description: Details of a single educational entry belonging to a specific resume. The document ID (`educationId`) must match the `id` field within the document. To ensure authorization independence, documents in this collection MUST include denormalized 'userId' and 'resumeId' fields, matching the respective path segments.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent resume.

params.name: resumeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent resume document.

params.name: educationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the education entry.

definition.description: Details of a single skill entry belonging to a specific resume. The document ID (`skillId`) must match the `id` field within the document. To ensure authorization independence, documents in this collection MUST include denormalized 'userId' and 'resumeId' fields, matching the respective path segments.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent resume.

params.name: resumeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent resume document.

params.name: skillId
params.description: The unique identifier for the skill entry.

definition.description: Stores structured results of an AI-powered interview analysis for a user. The document ID (`interviewAnalysisId`) must match the `id` field within the document. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, which must match the path's `userId` segment.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who initiated this interview analysis.

params.name: interviewAnalysisId
params.description: The unique identifier for the interview analysis document.

definition.description: Stores a single recruitment question and its extracted answer as part of an interview analysis. The document ID (`questionAnswerId`) must match the `id` field within the document. To ensure authorization independence, documents in this collection MUST include denormalized 'userId' and 'interviewAnalysisId' fields, matching the respective path segments.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent interview analysis.

params.name: interviewAnalysisId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent interview analysis document.

params.name: questionAnswerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the question and answer entry.

definition.description: Records payment transactions made by a user. The document ID (`paymentId`) must match the `id` field within the document. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, which must match the path's `userId` segment.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who made the payment.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the payment transaction.

definition.description: Logs requests made by a user to AI services for auditing and debugging. The document ID (`aiRequestId`) must match the `id` field within the document. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, which must match the path's `userId` segment.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who initiated the AI request.

params.name: aiRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier for the AI request log entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for 'RecruitedAI' (CareerCraft AI) prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering strictly to the core design principles and mandates. The primary strategy employed is **Authorization Independence via Denormalization** and **Structural Segregation**.

**Authorization Independence:**
All user-owned data is structured hierarchically under `/users/{userId}`. To eliminate the need for `get()` operations in Firestore Security Rules and enable atomic operations (like creating a parent and its sub-document in a single batch), the `userId` of the owner is denormalized into every document in subcollections (e.g., `resumes`, `interviewAnalyses`, `payments`, `aiRequests`).

Furthermore, for nested subcollections like `workExperiences`, `educations`, `skills` (under `resumes`), and `questionAnswers` (under `interviewAnalyses`), not only `userId` but also the direct parent's ID (`resumeId` or `interviewAnalysisId`) is denormalized. This means a `workExperience` document, for example, will explicitly contain `userId` and `resumeId`. This allows security rules to evaluate ownership and parentage directly from the document's own data (`resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` and `resource.data.resumeId == request.path[4]`), making rules atomic, simple, and robust. It prevents broken transactions where a parent document might not exist or be accessible during a child document's creation or update.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
The structural segregation and consistent denormalization directly support Query-Able Paths (QAPs). All user-specific data is naturally partitioned by the authenticated user's ID. When a client queries a collection like `/users/{request.auth.uid}/resumes`, the security rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `userId` segment in the path and the `userId` field denormalized in the `Resume` documents. This ensures that `list` operations implicitly filter data by the authenticated user, preventing accidental exposure of other users' data and allowing Firestore's security rules to act as an enforcement layer, not a filtering mechanism. For subcollections, the rules will similarly verify `userId` and the parent ID (e.g., `resumeId`) against the path and the document's denormalized fields, ensuring all returned documents conform to the specified security posture.

**Structural Segregation:**
All private, user-specific data is kept within the `/users/{userId}/` subtree. This ensures a homogeneous security posture: all documents within these collections are strictly owned by and accessible only to the `userId` specified in their path and document data. This avoids mixing public and private data in the same collection, simplifying security rules immensely.

**Public-Facing Data (Online Resume/LinkTree):**
As the provided schemas do not include entities for public-facing documents, and the design principles mandate structural segregation (no mixing public/private in a single collection), this structure assumes that public resume views or LinkTree content would be generated by a Firebase Cloud Function that reads the authenticated user's private data and serves a public-facing representation. If direct public Firestore access were required, new entity schemas for 'PublicResume' or 'LinkTreeProfile' would be created and placed in a top-level collection (e.g., `/publicResumes/{publicResumeId}`) with appropriate public read rules.

**Overall:**
This design leverages a deep, user-centric hierarchy with crucial data denormalization to enable highly secure, scalable, and easily debuggable Firestore security rules. It ensures that authorization logic is straightforward, relies solely on `request.auth.uid` and document data, and supports efficient querying while maintaining data integrity.
